title: From Ease To Difficulty
study: A Study In The Life Of Joseph
scriptureForWeek: GENESIS 41:1-36 Two full years later, Pharaoh dreamed that he was standing on the bank of the Nile River. In his dream he saw seven fat, healthy cows come up out of the river and begin grazing in the marsh grass. Then he saw seven more cows come up behind them from the Nile, but these were scrawny and thin. These cows stood beside the fat cows on the riverbank. Then the scrawny, thin cows ate the seven healthy, fat cows! At this point in the dream, Pharaoh woke up. But he fell asleep again and had a second dream. This time he saw seven heads of grain, plump and beautiful, growing on a single stalk. Then seven more heads of grain appeared, but these were shriveled and withered by the east wind. And these thin heads swallowed up the seven plump, well-formed heads! Then Pharaoh woke up again and realized it was a dream. The next morning Pharaoh was very disturbed by the dreams. So he called for all the magicians and wise men of Egypt. When Pharaoh told them his dreams, not one of them could tell him what they meant. Finally, the king’s chief cup-bearer spoke up. “Today I have been reminded of my failure,” he told Pharaoh. “Some time ago, you were angry with the chief baker and me, and you imprisoned us in the palace of the captain of the guard. One night the chief baker and I each had a dream, and each dream had its own meaning. There was a young Hebrew man with us in the prison who was a slave of the captain of the guard. We told him our dreams, and he told us what each of our dreams meant. And everything happened just as he had predicted. I was restored to my position as cup-bearer, and the chief baker was executed and impaled on a pole.” Pharaoh sent for Joseph at once, and he was quickly brought from the prison. After he shaved and changed his clothes, he went in and stood before Pharaoh. Then Pharaoh said to Joseph, “I had a dream last night, and no one here can tell me what it means. But I have heard that when you hear about a dream you can interpret it.” “It is beyond my power to do this,” Joseph replied. “But God can tell you what it means and set you at ease.”
weekTheme: Boys panic, men pray This week, we are looking at the difficulty of discernment. If a teenager is going to become a true man who can navigate the challenges of life with wisdom and skill, he is going to need to discern what the right thing to do is in hard and complex situations. Learning to hear the voice of God in the midst of all the confusion and chaos will set us up as men of clarity, truth and wisdom. Joseph had to learn to discern what God was saying while he was a prisoner in a strange land, and it led to his deliverance. God wants to help you become that kind of man, who can hear truth in a culture of lies, understand God's will when others are confused, and make decisions that advance your destiny with confidence.

days.title: The Difficulty Of Discernment
days.scripture: GENESIS 41:1-16 1 Two full years later, Pharaoh dreamed that he was standing on the bank of the Nile River. 2 In his dream he saw seven fat, healthy cows come up out of the river and begin grazing in the marsh grass. 3 Then he saw seven more cows come up behind them from the Nile, but these were scrawny and thin. These cows stood beside the fat cows on the riverbank. 4 Then the scrawny, thin cows ate the seven healthy, fat cows! At this point in the dream, Pharaoh woke up. 5 But he fell asleep again and had a second dream. This time he saw seven heads of grain, plump and beautiful, growing on a single stalk. 6 Then seven more heads of grain appeared, but these were shriveled and withered by the east wind. 7 And these thin heads swallowed up the seven plump, well-formed heads! Then Pharaoh woke up again and realized it was a dream. 8 The next morning Pharaoh was very disturbed by the dreams. So he called for all the magicians and wise men of Egypt. When Pharaoh told them his dreams, not one of them could tell him what they meant. 9 Finally, the king’s chief cup-bearer spoke up. “Today I have been reminded of my failure,” he told Pharaoh. 10 “Some time ago, you were angry with the chief baker and me, and you imprisoned us in the palace of the captain of the guard. 11 One night the chief baker and I each had a dream, and each dream had its own meaning. 12 There was a young Hebrew man with us in the prison who was a slave of the captain of the guard. We told him our dreams, and he told us what each of our dreams meant. 13 And everything happened just as he had predicted. I was restored to my position as cup-bearer, and the chief baker was executed and impaled on a pole.” 14 Pharaoh sent for Joseph at once, and he was quickly brought from the prison. After he shaved and changed his clothes, he went in and stood before Pharaoh. 15 Then Pharaoh said to Joseph, “I had a dream last night, and no one here can tell me what it means. But I have heard that when you hear about a dream you can interpret it.” 16 “It is beyond my power to do this,” Joseph replied. “But God can tell you what it means and set you at ease.”
days.reflection: The pressure on Joseph is heating up. He has been rejected, sold into slavery, lied to, and forgotten in prison. He has had to overcome the difficulty of conflict, circumstances, temptation, and delays; yet, these are going to pale in comparison to the pressure of this moment. Joseph is going to have to overcome the difficulty of discernment. Discernment is the ability to skillfully understand and judge the best thing to do in any given situation. It’s about wisdom and insight and the right choice in confusing circumstances. Joseph has been given a gift to discern the meaning of people’s dreams and see what is going to happen as a result of them. This isn’t like some whacky psychic thing; it's real. He does this first in prison with the king’s servants, and now something amazing is going to happen. He is going to be asked to interpret the dreams of Pharaoh, the leader of Egypt. Can you imagine being in a situation like this? The most powerful man in the world is having bizarre nightmares and thinks that God might be saying something to him. Then that person calls you up and you have to explain the dream to him. If it goes well, you get promoted; if it goes poorly, you get killed. This is the situation Joseph finds himself in. Joseph is facing the difficulty of discernment. Joseph is going to need God's help. You live in a time of history where you are going to have to learn how to discern things as well. You may not have to do it for kings or presidents, but you are going to have to learn to discern in ways that will really impact your life. You are going to have to discern what is true and what is false. What is real and what is fake. How people think about things and how God thinks about things. You are going to have to discern small things- like which snack you want to eat, healthy or junk. Mid-sized things- like which friends you can really trust or not. And massive things- like if the Bible is true or just a human book, or if you want to be a Christian or live for yourself. Joseph needed God's help to discern what was right, and you will too. The good news is God promises to guide us when we need help. John 16:13 says, “When the Spirit of truth comes, he will guide you into all truth. He will not speak on his own but will tell you what he has heard. He will tell you about the future.” The Holy Spirit is God’s gift to help us discern when things get hard. Look at how John puts this a little later on: “But you have received the Holy Spirit, and he lives within you, so you don’t need anyone to teach you what is true. For the Spirit teaches you everything you need to know, and what he teaches is true—it is not a lie. So just as he has taught you, remain in fellowship with Christ.” 1 John 2:26-27 God will help you figure out the best things to do when it gets hard. God will give you wisdom if you ask. God will help you discern what’s best, so you can become a wise young man. God did that for Joseph in a crazy situation, and he will do it for you too.
questions: Do you normally remember your dreams or not?
What’s one of the weirdest dreams you have ever had. Do you think it meant anything?
Is there something happening in your life right now where you need discernment? Anywhere you feel really confused and need God's help?
Ask the person you are doing this study with about how they learned to discern things. Ask them to give you an example of where God showed them what to do in difficult circumstances.

thoughts.verse: The LORD says, “I will guide you along the best pathway for your life. I will advise you and watch over you."
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment and bring any situation that is hard or confusing before God. Ask him to help you discern the right and best thing to do. Wait for a moment to see if he speaks anything to your heart and mind.

days.title: Getting Wisdom, Lots Of It
days.scripture: GENESIS 41:33-36 33 “Therefore, Pharaoh should find an intelligent and wise man and put him in charge of the entire land of Egypt. 34 Then Pharaoh should appoint supervisors over the land and let them collect one-fifth of all the crops during the seven good years. 35 Have them gather all the food produced in the good years that are just ahead and bring it to Pharaoh’s storehouses. Store it away, and guard it so there will be food in the cities. 36 That way there will be enough to eat when the seven years of famine come to the land of Egypt. Otherwise this famine will destroy the land.”
days.reflection: God has helped Joseph discern the most difficult moment of his life. He has just interpreted the dreams and future of a whole nation. But it's interesting to note what the need was after he interpreted the dream. “Find an intelligent and wise man and put him in charge of the entire land of Egypt.” If Egypt was going to move forward, it would need a wise man to get them there. This is a key insight. Discernment is about becoming wise. We need wise men in leadership. We need men who can figure out what to do. The solution to confusing times is wise men. The solution to the chaos our world faces is men of discernment. One of the most important things you need to do as you grow up is to become wise. This is because we live in a world filled with fools. The Bible talks a lot about the wise and the foolish and the difference between the two. It’s important that you don’t end up becoming a fool. Being a fool sucks. You get in trouble all the time, make bad decisions, lose influence, and destroy your future. You do not want to be a fool. The way to avoid that is by seeking wisdom. Seek to become a wise and intelligent man like Joseph who can discern what to do and be entrusted with responsibility. Here is how to do that: SEEK WISDOM LIKE CRAZY. Look at the importance of seeking to be wise in these verses: The discerning heart seeks knowledge, but the mouth of a fool feeds on folly. (Proverbs 15:14) The heart of the discerning acquires knowledge, for the ears of the wise seek it out. (Proverbs 18:15) You won’t become wise on accident. It can’t be downloaded into your life like a song is from Spotify. It requires a diligent and determined pursuit. You need to be asking yourself all the time: how do I become wise? How can I figure out what the wise thing to do is in this situation? As a young man today, you live in crazy and confusing times. The good news is, God will give you wisdom if you ask for it. He loves helping young men like you figure out how to be smart when others are being dumb. Look at the promise from this verse in James 1:5: If you need wisdom, ask our generous God, and he will give it to you. He will not rebuke you for asking. Get obsessed with being wise. Get obsessed with wisdom like people do with video games, sports, and girls. Do this, and your life will get really, really good.
questions: What is one of the most foolish things you can remember doing when you were young?
What is one of the wisest things you did?
Who is one of the wisest people you know? What makes them so wise?
In what ways do you see teenagers being foolish?
What are some practical ways that you think you could become wiser as a teenager?

thoughts.verse: My son, if you accept my words and store up my commands within you, turning your ear to wisdom and applying your heart to understanding— indeed, if you call out for insight and cry aloud for understanding, and if you look for it as for silver and search for it as for hidden treasure, then you will understand the fear of the LORD and find the knowledge of God.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to give you a hunger for wisdom and hatred for foolishness. Then ask him to show you where you can be wise today.

days.title: Discernment From God'S Word
days.scripture: GENESIS 41:14-16 14 Pharaoh sent for Joseph at once, and he was quickly brought from the prison. After he shaved and changed his clothes, he went in and stood before Pharaoh. 15 Then Pharaoh said to Joseph, “I had a dream last night, and no one here can tell me what it means. But I have heard that when you hear about a dream you can interpret it.” 16 “It is beyond my power to do this,” Joseph replied. “But God can tell you what it means and set you at ease.”
days.reflection: If you want to be a wise young man, you have to learn to hear from God. Joseph knew this. He needed wisdom from outside himself to interpret Pharaoh’s dream. He didn’t rely on his opinion or “his truth”, or what the people around him said. He needed God's word for guidance. Just like Joseph, you are going to need to rely on what God has said. If you listen to what the media says, You-tubers say, or what your friends at school say, you will miss out on the most helpful and wise opinion of all - God's. But it's easy to hear lots of stuff about God and still not be smart with God. You need to move beyond just hearing basic stuff about God and go on to the deep stuff about God. The best way to learn from God is to understand his word. And the best way to understand God's word is to get passionate about reading it. God’s word is alive and can speak to the struggles we have in amazing ways. Hebrews 4:12 says that “The word of God is alive and active. Sharper than any double-edged sword, it penetrates even to dividing soul and spirit, joints and marrow; it judges the thoughts and attitudes of the heart.” Have you thought about the Bible like that? Not as a boring book but as a living book? Something that can get inside your head and hearts and help you discern what to do. Have you thought about the Bible like a double-edged sword? The Bible isn’t a boring text book. It's an amazing story that opens up a world of wisdom better than any movie or video game out there. But we have to stay hungry for wisdom and keep learning. We have to keep growing and graduate from spiritual baby food to spiritual meat. Lots of young guys fall into the trap of a basic understanding about God but not wise growth in God. Hebrews 5:11-14 goes on to say, "There is much more we would like to say about this, but it is difficult to explain, especially since you are spiritually dull and don’t seem to listen. You have been believers so long now that you ought to be teaching others. Instead, you need someone to teach you again the basic things about God’s word. You are like babies who need milk and cannot eat solid food. For someone who lives on milk is still an infant and doesn’t know how to do what is right. Solid food is for those who are mature, who through training have the skill to recognize the difference between right and wrong." Can you imagine going out to a nice steak restaurant and sitting next a couple who show up at the same time as you. And then, to your shock, instead of eating steak, the guy pulls out a bottle of milk and starts sucking on the bottle while his wife eats ribeye. You would think that it was weird and immature. Yet, many men do this with wisdom. They never learn to discern what God really says, and so they remain like spiritual babies. God’s word will give you everything you need to become truly wise and move from baby food to meat. You are already on your way. You are here doing this study, aren’t you? Way to go. Stay hungry.
questions: Who do you think people your age turn to for advice and wisdom? Do you think this is helping them to become truly wise or not? Why?
If you could describe how you see the Bible in 3 words, what would they be? (No wrong answers, honest ones only)

Joseph said that when you hear from God, you will know what things mean and you will be set at ease. Has anything from God's word ever helped make sense of things for you, or put you at ease? If so, what was it?

thoughts.verse: Oh, how I love your instructions! I think about them all day long. Your commands make me wiser than my enemies, for they are my constant guide. Yes, I have more insight than my teachers, for I am always thinking of your laws. I am even wiser than my elders, for I have kept your commandments.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to help you like the Bible and really learn to value it. Ask him to make you hungry for wisdom from his word.

days.title: Discerning The 4 Voices
days.scripture: GENESIS 40:5-8, 41:8-12 5 While they were in prison, Pharaoh’s cup-bearer and baker each had a dream one night, and each dream had its own meaning. 6 When Joseph saw them the next morning, he noticed that they both looked upset. 7 “Why do you look so worried today?” he asked them. 8 And they replied, “We both had dreams last night, but no one can tell us what they mean.” “Interpreting dreams is God’s business,” Joseph replied. “Go ahead and tell me your dreams.” 8 The next morning Pharaoh was very disturbed by the dreams. So he called for all the magicians and wise men of Egypt. When Pharaoh told them his dreams, not one of them could tell him what they meant.9 Finally, the king’s chief cup-bearer spoke up. “Today I have been reminded of my failure,” he told Pharaoh. 10 “Some time ago, you were angry with the chief baker and me, and you imprisoned us in the palace of the captain of the guard. 11 One night the chief baker and I each had a dream, and each dream had its own meaning. 12 There was a young Hebrew man with us in the prison who was a slave of the captain of the guard. We told him our dreams, and he told us what each of our dreams meant.
days.reflection: It’s hard to know who to trust, what to believe, and how to make sense of all the messages the world is telling us. Everyone claims to be right, everyone disagrees, and so many people are fighting about truth. There are lots of voices out there causing confusion. You probably see this happening at school, online, around your friends. So many opinions with so much intensity. Joseph had to learn to hear God in the midst of all the confusion of his world. This showed up when it came to interpreting dreams. Remember in prison how the king's servants couldn’t discern what their own dreams meant? Pharaoh couldn’t make sense of his dreams, and as you read above, none of the magicians or wise men in Egypt could figure out how to discern the truth. Joseph, however, had learned to hear from God in the midst of the confusion, and you will need to discern God's voice amidst all the confusion in your world too. When trying to hear from God, it’s important to remember that there may be lots of other voices clamoring to be heard as well. Discerning which voice is speaking in any situation is one of the keys to hearing God's voice. There are 4 main voices to discern in any situation: SELF Sometimes when we are seeking wisdom, we are just listening to ourselves. It’s our own thoughts and opinions popping into our head telling us what to do. When we only listen to what we want, we rely on our wisdom, not God's. As cool as you are, you don’t want all your decisions about life processed through the brain of a male teenager. WORLD It’s amazing how much of the world’s ideas can shape our thinking, opinions and values. From little memes to big ideas, the world is working to make us think about life, money, sex, fun, and right and wrong from its perspective. You need to make sure the voices of the world don’t tell you what to do. SATAN You have to realize that you have a spiritual enemy who hates you and seeks to destroy you. Satan is called the father of lies, and he wants to distort your thoughts so that you don’t do what God wants. You have to learn to know the way he seeks to trick us and trap us. He used half-truths and verses out of context when trying to tempt Jesus. Be on your guard against half-truths from hell. GOD God promises to speak to you and guide you. His voice sounds like comforting and challenging love. He will show you what is right according to his wisdom. His insight and help is better than relying on yourself and better than worldly wisdom. It protects us from the lies of the enemy, and leads us to a path of peace. Isaiah 30:21 says, "Your own ears will hear him. Right behind you a voice will say, 'This is the way you should go,' whether to the right or to the left." Learning to listen to that voice is so important. Joseph did. You can. If you tune your ear, God's voice will be the loudest of the 4 voices in your life.
questions: Are there any issues in your life right now that you are a little bit confused about, or have questions about? Any questions like, “Why does the Bible teach that, or why does God think this?”
Look over the 4 voices (Self, World, Satan, God). Can you think about a time you have heard any of these voices when trying to make an important decision?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share a time they have heard each of the 4 voices.

thoughts.verse: All Scripture is inspired by God and is useful to teach us what is true and to make us realize what is wrong in our lives. It corrects us when we are wrong and teaches us to do what is right. God uses it to prepare and equip his people to do every good work.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to train your ear to hear his voice. Ask him to help you discern lies from the world and the evil one and to increase the volume of God in your life.

days.title: Don'T Panic, Pray
days.scripture: GENESIS 41:14-16 14 Pharaoh sent for Joseph at once, and he was quickly brought from the prison. After he shaved and changed his clothes, he went in and stood before Pharaoh. 15 Then Pharaoh said to Joseph, “I had a dream last night, and no one here can tell me what it means. But I have heard that when you hear about a dream you can interpret it.” 16 “It is beyond my power to do this,” Joseph replied. “But God can tell you what it means and set you at ease.”
days.reflection: When you don’t know what to do, it creates anxiety and fear. You can become worried that you are going to make the wrong decision and screw up your life. You can also feel paralyzed because you don’t know how to move forward. You can be worried about what your friends or parents will think of you if you make a dumb decision. The good news is that God wants to help you discern what’s best, so you can get rid of your anxiety and have deep peace in your heart. Look at what Joseph promised Pharaoh when he couldn’t discern his dreams: “But God can tell you what it means and set you at ease.” God's voice doesn’t just make us wise; it gives us peace. It sets us at ease and removes our stress. This peace is like a secret weapon in our hearts when everyone around us is freaking out. That’s why it’s important to pray when we are confused or overwhelmed. Prayer is simply inviting God to help us when we don’t know what to do. And praying to God comes with a promise. Philippians 4:6-7 says, “Don’t worry about anything; instead, pray about everything. Tell God what you need, and thank him for all he has done. Then you will experience God’s peace, which exceeds anything we can understand. His peace will guard your hearts and minds as you live in Christ Jesus.” It says that God will give you peace, and that peace guards your heart and mind against attacks from confusion. It’s like having soldiers standing at the door of your heart and head, fighting off fear, worry, anxiety and confusion when they attack how you think and feel. God will put a barrier between the chaos of what’s happening around you so you function with confidence and courage. And this peace doesn’t just protect us, it directs us. Colossians 3:15 says, “And let the peace that comes from Christ rule in your hearts. For as members of one body you are called to live in peace.” The word rule means “to be an umpire, to decide, determine.” That peace helps you know what God wants. Like an umpire in a game, this peace can let you know what God wants by calling, “out,” “safe” etc. on the decisions you are trying to make. It’s one of the ways God helps us discern what to do. If we pray and ask him, he will put a strange and supernatural peace in us that is hard to explain to others but makes perfect sense to us. Whatever you need help figuring out, commit these things to God in prayer. It can be a short, simple prayer like, “God, help, I’m stuck and I need you” or a longer prayer where you really pour out your heart. But be confident of this: when you invite God in to help you discern what to do, he promises his peace will come to you. Joseph found peace in prison and betrayal; God will help you find peace where you need it too. Why not start bringing the things you need to figure out to him today?
questions: Have you ever had a moment in church, or youth group, or reading the Bible when you have felt a strange kind of peace? If so, what was it?
Are there any places in your life right now where you are feeling anxiety or fear? Would you be willing to share them, and why they are stressful?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share a time with you where they experienced God's supernatural peace in a hard situation because they prayed.

thoughts.verse: You will keep in perfect peace all who trust in you, all whose thoughts are fixed on you! Trust in the LORD always, for the LORD GOD is the eternal Rock.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to bring the places you have fear and anxiety before God. Ask him to give you his supernatural peace to comfort you.

extras: WEEK 6 MANSCHOOL MEAT OR MILK MANSCHOOL REMINDER: "The recipe for fun is pretty simple when you’re raising boys: add to any activity an element of danger, stir in a little exploration, add a dash of destruction, and you’ve got yourself a winner." John Eldredge OPTION 1. BABY FOOD AND STEAK To highlight the importance of making sure that you grow from being a child in understanding God's word to an adult who can handle meat, we have a food event. Go and buy some baby food (vegetables and fruit). Get some formula too. Buy a couple of amazing steaks (or chicken or pork chops or burgers). Read through his verse again: There is much more we would like to say about this, but it is difficult to explain, especially since you are spiritually dull and don’t seem to listen. You have been believers so long now that you ought to be teaching others. Instead, you need someone to teach you again the basic things about God’s word. You are like babies who need milk and cannot eat solid food. For someone who lives on milk is still an infant and doesn’t know how to do what is right. Solid food is for those who are mature, who through training have the skill to recognize the difference between right and wrong. Hebrews 5:11-14 Make some baby milk and drink it - at least a sip of it together. Open the baby food and eat at least a mouthful of each kind. Stop and talk about what it was like. (Anticipate massive disgust here ☺) Then cook up the steaks and enjoy an amazing meal.
FIELD NOTES PRAYER FOR MY SON
